instructions:
Write `.docs-ops/extractors/flutter-extractor.py` — a regex-based Dart public-API extractor — and run it to produce `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/flutter.json`.

MODEL: Mirror the structure and output-schema conventions of `.docs-ops/extractors/android-extractor.py` (the Kotlin/Java extractor written in task 0011). Same approach: pure-Python, no external deps, walks the SDK source, emits a JSON surface.

SDK ROOT: `Amity-Social-Cloud-SDK-Flutter-Internal/` (sibling of `social-plus-docs/`).

SCOPE — what to scan:
  - INCLUDE all `.dart` files under `lib/` (the standard public source location for a Dart package).
  - EXCLUDE: `test/`, `example/`, `examples/`, `build/`, `.dart_tool/`, anything under `.git`, generated files (e.g. `*.g.dart`, `*.freezed.dart` if any).
  - You can use `find Amity-Social-Cloud-SDK-Flutter-Internal/lib -name '*.dart'` filtered with the excludes.

WHAT COUNTS AS PUBLIC API:
  - **Dart visibility rule**: a name is PRIVATE iff it starts with an underscore (`_Foo`, `_internalMethod`, `_field`). Anything NOT starting with underscore is library-public. There is no `public` / `private` keyword in Dart.
  - For library-private vs package-public: Dart has 'library private' (the underscore rule), and re-exports via `export 'src/foo.dart'` from the package's main barrel determine what's package-public. v1: capture all underscore-free top-level declarations across `lib/` — the main barrel (probably `lib/amity_sdk.dart` or similar) will narrow this later if needed.

DECLARATION KINDS to detect:

  Type-level:
    `class <Name>`, `abstract class <Name>`, `mixin <Name>`, `extension <Name> on <T>`, `enum <Name>`, `typedef <Name>` (function type alias)
  Member-level:
    Methods: `<ReturnType> methodName(...)`, `void methodName(...)`, `Future<T> methodName(...)`, `Stream<T> methodName(...)`, plus `static` variants and operator overloads
    Properties: `final <Type> name`, `var <Type>? name`, `<Type> get name`, `set name(<Type>)`, `late final <Type> name`, `static const`
    Constructors: `ClassName(...)`, `ClassName.namedConstructor(...)`, `factory ClassName(...)`, `const ClassName(...)`
    Enum values: bare identifiers inside `enum { ... }` body — like Kotlin enum entries.
  Top-level functions and constants:
    `<ReturnType> functionName(...)`, `void functionName(...)` at file top level
    `const <Type> NAME = ...`, `final <Type> name = ...` at file top level

IMPORTANT DART-SPECIFIC NOTES:
  - Annotations (`@override`, `@deprecated`, `@JsonSerializable()`, `@freezed`, etc.) appear above declarations. Strip them when looking for the visibility/kind keyword.
  - Dart's `part of` files: a `.dart` file starting with `part of` is a fragment of another library. Still walk these, but record `is_part_of` in the entry for clarity.
  - Dart extensions: `extension AmityChannelExt on AmityChannel { ... }` — track separately like Swift extensions. Members aggregate into the host type's member list, marked `from_extension=true`, OR if host type is not in the surface, keep as its own entry under `extensions[]`.
  - Mixins: track as a separate kind, similar to interfaces in Android.

OUTPUT SCHEMA — write to `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/flutter.json`. Follow android.json conventions:

{
  "extractor_version": "0.1.0",
  "extractor": "flutter-extractor.py",
  "extracted_at": "<iso>",
  "sdk_product": "amity_sdk",
  "sdk_source_roots": ["Amity-Social-Cloud-SDK-Flutter-Internal/lib"],
  "types": {
    "AmityCoreClient": {
      "kind": "class | abstract_class | enum | typedef",
      "primary_decl": { "file": "...", "line": N },
      "members": [
        { "name": "setup", "kind": "func | getter | setter | field | const | constructor | named_constructor | factory | enum_value", "file": "...", "line": N, "signature_hint": "<raw trimmed line>", "from_extension": false }
      ],
      "extensions": [ { "name": "AmityCoreClientExt", "file": "...", "line": N } ]
    }
  },
  "mixins": { "<name>": { ... same shape ... } },
  "global_funcs": [ ... ],
  "global_consts": [ ... ],
  "typedefs": [ ... ],
  "unhandled": [],
  "stats": {
    "files_scanned": <int>,
    "part_of_files": <int>,
    "types": <int>,
    "mixins": <int>,
    "members_total": <int>,
    "global_funcs": <int>,
    "global_consts": <int>,
    "unhandled_lines": <int>
  }
}

IMPORTANT — v1 scope is the same restraint as android-extractor.py and ios-extractor.py: capture NAME + KIND + FILE + LINE + raw `signature_hint`. Parameter / return-type parsing is OUT OF SCOPE.

LEARN FROM ANDROID-EXTRACTOR'S PITFALLS:
  - The Android v1 had inflated `global_funcs` count because the per-line method regex fired on indented class methods. AVOID this in Flutter — only count a method as `global_funcs` if it's at column-0 indentation AND outside any `{...}` brace scope. Track brace depth like ios-extractor.py does.

After writing, RUN it. Expected order of magnitude: the Flutter SDK is smaller than iOS/Android — probably hundreds of types, low thousands of members. Verify the output is non-empty and reasonable.

Report in `notes`: stats, 3-5 sample type entries, top 10 types by member count, list of unhandled patterns. Confirm `global_funcs` is NOT inflated (sanity-check: <100 for an SDK of this size is reasonable).

validators.name: extractor_runs_and_produces_valid_surface
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/extractors/flutter-extractor.py > /dev/null && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/sdk-surface/flutter.json')); assert 'types' in d and 'stats' in d; assert d['stats']['types']>0 and d['stats']['members_total']>0, f'unexpectedly empty surface: {d[\"stats\"]}'; print('OK')"
